card.intuition: A way to share data like 'Current User' or 'Theme' across the entire component tree without having to manually pass props down through every single level (Prop Drilling).
card.analogy: A radio station: instead of the parent shouting the news to the child, who whispers it to the grandchild (Prop Drilling), the parent simply broadcasts the news on a specific frequency (Provider). Any component that wants to hear it just tunes in (useContext).
when_to_use: Sharing localized strings (i18n) across the app
gotchas: Context is NOT a replacement for all state management! Every time the context value changes, EVERY component consuming that context re-renders
Avoid putting deep, complex state objects into a single context; split them into multiple smaller contexts to prevent wasted renders

steps.label: Create Context
steps.annotation: Context object created with default value.

steps.label: Provider
steps.annotation: Provider wraps subtree and supplies the current value.

steps.label: Consumer
steps.annotation: Any descendant reads value via useContext — no prop drilling.

steps.label: Update
steps.annotation: Provider value changes — all consumers re-render.

steps.label: Optimise
steps.annotation: Split contexts by update frequency to prevent unnecessary re-renders.

exercise.prompt: Identify the two main components of the Context API pattern.
exercise.starter_code:
components = [
    "1. The ________: Wraps segments of the tree and provides the value",
    "2. The hook __________: Used by child components to read the value"
]
exercise.test_code:
assert "Provider" in components[0]
assert "useContext" in components[1]
print("PASS")
